text.title: Figma AI Plugin Inventory
text.summary: Top Figma AI plugins, their capabilities, agent-friendliness, and hard limitations.

list.item: Magician — AI-powered icon and image generation; in-plugin only; no headless API; outputs vary per run
Automator — batch operations with AI macros (rename, detach, resize); JS macro runner; no external trigger
Content Reel — AI text and image placeholders from data files; data files can be pre-generated by agent
Stark — accessibility checking (contrast, color blind simulation); paid API for CI contrast checks only
Similayer — smart layer selection by similarity; no API surface
Diagram — AI diagramming inside Figma canvas; no API; AI diagramming in-plugin only

rule.statement: Agents cannot invoke Figma plugins headlessly. The Figma plugin sandbox is browser-JS only — no server-accessible API exists.
rule.rationale: Figma's plugin architecture runs code in a browser-sandboxed iframe. There is no REST API or CLI for triggering plugins externally. Agents that propose API-driven plugin automation are hallucinating.

rule.statement: Agent role is pre-processing (generate data files, configuration scripts) and post-processing (analyze Figma JSON exports). Humans run plugins in Figma.
rule.rationale: This boundary is firm and determined by the Figma plugin sandbox architecture. Designing workflows that respect this boundary avoids wasted engineering effort.

antipattern.description: Relying on AI plugin outputs (Magician, etc.) for deterministic pipeline results.
antipattern.reason: AI plugin outputs vary with each run; reproducibility is poor and not suitable for deterministic design system workflows.

antipattern.description: Using Stark plugin as the only accessibility validation step.
antipattern.reason: Stark flags contrast issues but cannot audit interaction flow or focus order; it is a partial check only.

antipattern.description: Running Automator batch operations on the production Figma file without testing on a duplicate first.
antipattern.reason: Batch operations at scale can break component relationships; test on a duplicate file to validate behavior before applying to production.

rule.statement: Set brand guidelines (color variables, typography styles) in Figma before running any generative plugin.
rule.rationale: AI plugin outputs anchor to existing styles when present; without them, generative outputs default to generic aesthetics that require rework.

rule.statement: Document which content in a file is AI-generated with a Figma annotation.
rule.rationale: AI-generated content must be distinguishable from human-authored content for future audits, brand reviews, and accessibility checks.
